architecture_summary: ViLBERT extends BERT to a multi-modal two-stream model that processes visual and textual inputs in separate streams and lets them interact through co-attentional transformer layers. It pretrains on Conceptual Captions with masked multi-modal modelling and multi-modal alignment prediction, then transfers to vision-and-language tasks with only minor task-specific heads.

benchmark_results.table_caption: Transfer task results for our ViLBERT model compared with existing state-of-the-art and sensible architectural ablations. â€  indicates models without pretraining on Conceptual Captions. For VCR and VQA which have private test sets, we report test results (in parentheses) only for our full model. Our full ViLBERT model outperforms task-specific state-of-the-art models across all tasks.

methods_used: linear decay learning rate schedule with warm up
novel_contributions: a multi-modal two-stream extension of BERT for vision-and-language
novel co-attentional transformer layers for cross-modal interaction
pretraining on Conceptual Captions with masked multi-modal modelling and multi-modal alignment prediction
a simple transfer setup that achieves state-of-the-art on four established vision-and-language tasks

key_claims.claim: ViLBERT extends BERT to a two-stream vision-and-language model with co-attentional transformer layers that enable sparse interaction between modalities.
The model is pretrained on Conceptual Captions using two proxy tasks: masked multi-modal modelling and multi-modal alignment prediction.
ViLBERT achieves state-of-the-art on all four transferred tasks: visual question answering, visual commonsense reasoning, referring expressions, and caption-based image retrieval.
The paper reports that the full ViLBERT model outperforms task-specific state-of-the-art models across all tasks.
The two-stream architecture improves performance over a single-stream model, and pretraining on Conceptual Captions further improves results.
Increasing the depth of the visual stream helps some tasks such as VQA and image retrieval, while other tasks benefit from shallower models.
Performance improves monotonically as more of the Conceptual Captions dataset is used during pretraining.
